summary: Frontend testing of Quest Hero user flows. Custom story CREATION succeeds at the API level (backend verified: 7 stories incl. 'The Test Quest' after POST), but the new story does NOT appear in the Stories list UI after navigating back from /create-story — BLOCKING the rest of the flow (start story → tasks → beat advancement → AI image gen). Identified root cause: missing focus-based refresh on stories screen. Could not complete steps 5-13 because the custom story never became selectable from UI.

ui_bugs.issue: Stories list is not refreshed when returning from /create-story. The newly created custom story does not appear until the user manually pull-to-refresh OR fully reloads the app. Confirmed: POST /api/stories/custom returned 200 and DB had the story, but UI still showed only 6 built-in stories.

integration_issues.flow: Create custom story → return to Stories list → start it
integration_issues.issue: Backend creates story successfully but frontend never re-fetches list on screen focus, so user cannot find/start the story they just created. Blocks every downstream test step (start adventure, task victory advancement, villain image gen).

action_items: Fix /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/stories.tsx: replace `useEffect(() => loadStories(), [])` with a focus-aware loader. Use `useFocusEffect(useCallback(() => { loadStories(); }, []))` from expo-router (or @react-navigation/native). This will reload the stories list every time the Stories tab gains focus — including after router.back() from /create-story.
Alternative/additional fix: in create-story.tsx, after successful create, call `router.replace('/(tabs)/stories')` with a refresh param, or use a global event/context refresh.

Consider applying the same focus-refresh pattern to (tabs)/index.tsx (active story state may become stale) and (tabs)/profile.tsx (streak/stats) so newly completed tasks reflect immediately when switching tabs.
After fix, re-run full flow: create custom story → verify CUSTOM tag visible → Start Adventure → 3 tasks → victory modal on 3rd → Generate AI Image → cache test (refresh).
critical_code_review_comments: stories.tsx loads stories only on mount; with expo-router tab navigation, the tab screen is NOT unmounted on push/back to /create-story, so loadStories() never re-runs. This is the root cause.

Same pattern (useEffect with [] deps) used in tasks.tsx and profile.tsx via useApp() context, but those rely on refreshAll() which is at least called via onComplete/onCreate handlers, so they're less affected.
Suggest centralizing stories/state in the AppContext so create-story.tsx can trigger context.refreshStories() before router.back().

success_rate.backend: 100% (verified via direct API: POST /api/stories/custom + GET /api/stories both work)
success_rate.frontend: ~25% (3 of 13 requested checks passed: Home loads, 6 stories display on Stories tab, Create Story screen accepts input and submits. Blocked from validating remaining 10 steps because new story doesn't appear in UI.)

seed_data_creation: Created test custom story 'The Test Quest' (id: custom-1779254950.639375); deleted via DELETE /api/stories/custom/{id} during cleanup. No residual seed data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=https://epic-daily-1.preview.emergentagent.com. Backend custom-story create endpoint works (POST /api/stories/custom). The blocking bug is in /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/stories.tsx — list never refreshes on screen focus. Once fixed with useFocusEffect, re-run the full E2E flow. Note: react-native-web does NOT expose role='tab' on Expo Router tab bar buttons reliably — use testID-based selectors for Home/Tasks/Stories/Profile tabs (consider adding testID='tab-home', 'tab-tasks', etc. in (tabs)/_layout.tsx).
rca_of_issue: REPRODUCTION: (1) Open app, go to Stories tab, (2) Tap 'Custom' top-right -> /create-story, (3) Fill title='The Test Quest', description, leave Fantasy + 3 Acts default, fill 3 villains, (4) Tap 'Create Story', (5) Dismiss success Alert (auto navigates back via router.back()). EXPECTED: New story with CUSTOM tag visible in Stories list. ACTUAL: Stories list still shows only the 6 built-in stories. VERIFIED via direct API call: GET /api/stories returns 7 stories including the just-created one. ROOT CAUSE: stories.tsx line 26-28 — useEffect(() => { loadStories(); }, []) only fires on initial mount; Expo Router tab routes are kept alive during stack push/back, so the effect never re-runs. MITIGATION: use useFocusEffect to refetch on every focus, OR call refreshAll/loadStories via shared context after successful create.
